Driving variable: Mobile phase composition (aqueous methanol, acetone, acetonitrile)
Non-critical species behavior: The non-critical block elutes in LAC mode (retention increasing exponentially) or SEC mode depending on the critical block
Source section: Strategies in the separation of block copolymers
Source text: If one of the interaction parameters (c A or c B ) equals zero, the corresponding block becomes 'chromatographically invisible', and a separation according to the other block can be achieved (liquid chromatography under critical conditions: LCCC).

Driving variable: Mobile phase composition (aqueous methanol, acetone, or acetonitrile)
Non-critical species behavior: The less polar PO block elutes in LAC mode
Source section: Strategies in the separation of block copolymers
Source text: On many reversed phase columns, critical conditions for the (more polar) EO block have been found in aqueous methanol, acetone, or acetonitrile. In such a system, the less polar PO block elutes in LAC mode.

System: Poloxamers on reversed phase columns (high organic)
Driving variable: Mobile phase composition (higher content of organic solvent)
Non-critical species behavior: EO will elute in SEC mode
Source section: Strategies in the separation of block copolymers
Source text: One may, however, also find critical conditions for the PO unit on a RP column in a different mobile phase composition (with a higher content of organic solvent): In this case EO will elute in SEC mode.

Driving variable: Mobile phase composition (implied by column type)
Non-critical species behavior: At CAP for EO, PO elutes in SEC mode; at CAP for PO, EO elutes in LAC mode
Source section: Strategies in the separation of block copolymers
Source text: The opposite may happen on normal phase columns: at the CAP for the EO unit, PO will elute in SEC mode, while EO will elute in LAC mode at the CAP for PO (if such critical points can be found).

System: block copolymers of ethylene oxide (EO) and propylene oxide (PO)
Type of criticality: block criticality (one block elutes in exclusion mode, other at critical condition)
Non-critical species behavior: the non-critical block elutes in exclusion mode, allowing independent determination of molar mass distribution
Source text: Under these conditions, the other (non-critical) block elutes in exclusion mode, which allows an independent determination of the molar mass distribution of the individual blocks.
